rationale: Both correctly validate the direct-vs-delegated loader distinction and both independently surface the real latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling removed get_cache_scale) with file/line precision, so gap_hit is true for both. X duplicates its one big finding across two entries (test-file and production-file framed as separate 'major' items), pads with low-value stylistic nits (asking for a comment matching another file's), and never engages with the ground truth's actual non-blocking concern about the test's weak checkpoint validation. Y verifies the design against real upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader, fp8.py) rather than just the diff, lands on the same APPROVE verdict the humans reached, and its own non-blocking finding (the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader and doesn't prove the real mapping behavior) is a sharper, better-grounded cousin of akshatvishu's 'test doesn't fully close the loop' concern. Y is more precise, less redundant, and slightly more actionable overall.
